# UC-19.1.29 · Blade Server ECC Memory Error Rate (Cisco UCS)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Performance &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the little memory chips on each server for a slowly rising stream of small fixable errors, and we pair that with the bigger notices when something is truly wrong, so the repair team can change the right part in a quiet window. That is a different job from a general “something broke” list, and different from watching network wires or room air heat.*

---

## Description

Correlates **UCSM** `faultInst` **ECC**-family **cause**/**code** with **DIMM**-anchored per-poll **cumulative** **correctable** and **uncorrectable** **counters** from `memoryUnitEnvStats` / `memoryArrayEnvStats` in `cisco:ucs:stats`, parses a stable `dimm_dn` from the managed-object `dn` (`…/board/memarray-N/mem-M`), and converts counter growth into **per-hour** **correctable** / **uncorrectable** **rates** using **binned** `_time` and **`streamstats`** with the `if(curr>=prev, …)` **reset** leg for **clear-stats** and poll discontinuities. Unlike **UC-19.1.1** (a broad `faultInst` health grid bucketed from `dn` into CPU vs memory vs disk), this is a **DIMM**-level **error-velocity** detector: the **streamstats** path on **stats** answers “which `mem-` is climbing?” while a **`join`**ed **fault** arm counts **DIMM**-scoped **F0185** / **F0186** / **F0461**-family rows per `dimm_dn` in the same window. A **second** **`streamstats`** on **`corr_rate_per_hour`** supports **correctable_baseline_drift** against lookup-backed `corr_rate_per_hour_warn` / `crit` rows. **Not** a fabric **CRC** leg counter (see **19.1.13**) and **not** thermal (see **19.1.34**).

## Value

When a single **DIMM** moves from a rare single-bit pattern to a **correctable** storm or the first **uncorrectable** bank event, the business risk is a surprise maintenance bridge with guest crash or restart instead of a planned FRU during a change window. Joining **counter** **velocity** to **fault** **volume** gives platform and hardware teams lead time to **evacuate** hosts, line up a **DIMM** **RMA** before **SLA** clocks start for applications, and hand the field tech a concrete **`dimm_dn`** and slot before memory bandwidth collapses or **UCE**-class events eject a node from a stretched cluster. The outcome is predictable, budgeted **hardware** work versus a multi-VM incident.

## Implementation

Install **Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs** on a Heavy Forwarder; enable the UCS **fault** and **statistics** inputs so `cisco:ucs:faults` and `cisco:ucs:stats` land in `cisco_ucs` (or your org index) with `faultInst`, `memoryUnitEnvStats`, and `memoryArrayEnvStats` in the MO class list; add `local/transforms.conf` and `lookups/ucs_dimm_error_thresholds.csv` on Search Heads; verify `classId` coverage; then save the `spl` as a scheduled **alert** (15m for **imminent**; digest for **elevated** per `dimm_health`) with throttle on `dimm_dn`. The **subsearch** repeats `earliest=-24h@h` on `cisco:ucs:faults` so the saved search honors the same dual-sourcetype scope as a single `OR` in **dataSources** even though the primary pipeline starts on **stats**.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Differentiation (locked): **UC-19.1.1** is the broad **faultInst** operator grid—every blade and rack fault bucketed from `dn` by component (CPU, memory, disk, and so on). **UC-19.1.29** is the DIMM-focused ECC velocity detector that joins per-DIMM stat counter deltas to fault rows on a shared `dimm_dn`. 19.1.1 answers “what is broken right now?”; 19.1.29 answers “which `mem-` is climbing before an unrecoverable event?”
- Differentiation (locked): **UC-19.1.13** reuses the same **streamstats** + `if(curr>=prev, curr-prev, curr)` binned counter idiom for FI CRC / FCS legs. 19.1.29 applies the same reset clause to `memoryUnitEnvStats` / `memoryArrayEnvStats` correctable and uncorrectable gauges—a different counter family, same engineering philosophy.
- Differentiation (locked): **UC-19.1.34** is chassis thermal runaway (°C per hour, fan RPM correlation). This UC is a DIMM memory-counter rate-of-rise pattern—MO path and sensor class differ; do not merge the runbooks.
- Install Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs on a Heavy Forwarder and Search Heads; read-only UCSM API user; 443 TLS to the UCSM VIP; `cisco_ucs` in RBAC; know Intersight-first estates (see `exclusions`).

**Step 1 — Configure data collection** (literal `inputs.conf` template, enable classes, post-poll validation)
(1) From `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/default/inputs.conf`, copy the Cisco UCS fault and Cisco UCS statistics stanzas into `local/inputs.conf`. Set `disabled=0`, `index=cisco_ucs` (or your standard), credentials, and ensure the MO list includes at least faultInst, memoryUnitEnvStats, and memoryArrayEnvStats per your TA build’s wording. Restart only as release notes require.
(2) Verify `splunkd.log` for successful UCSM HTTPS polls without `CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED` or 401/403.
(3) Post-poll: `index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" | stats count by classId` must list memoryUnitEnvStats and memoryArrayEnvStats. `index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults" | head 3` must show `cause`/`code` on faultInst rows.
(4) Event volume: every enabled DIMM object on every poll drives license; the 15m bin in the `spl` smooths API cadence—do not shrink the bin below what your NOC can action without a false storm.

**Step 2 — Lookup setup** (`transforms.conf` + `**ucs_dimm_error_thresholds**.csv` stub, 6–8 rows, planning values)
(1) In your SH-owned app, add:
```ini
[ucs_dimm_error_thresholds]
filename = ucs_dimm_error_thresholds.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 1
default_match = UCS_DEFAULT
max_matches = 1
```
(2) Seed CSV (edit with hardware and Cisco TAM; values are planning, not warranty):
```csv
blade_model,dimm_size_gb,corr_rate_per_hour_warn,corr_rate_per_hour_crit,uncorr_rate_per_hour_crit,notes
B200_M5,16,80,800,0,DDR4 16G M5; tune
B200_M5,32,90,900,0,DDR4 32G pack-out
B200_M6,64,110,1100,0,DDR4 64G; more dies
B200_M7,32,100,1000,0,DDR5 32G
B200_M7,64,120,1200,0,DDR5 64G; storm band up
B200_M7,128,150,1500,0,High chip count
PMEM_STANDIN,0,200,2000,0,PMem placeholder—not conventional DIMM ECC
UCS_DEFAULT,0,100,1000,0,Fallback; replace with CMDB join
```
(3) Production: join `blade_model`+`dimm_size_gb` from a CMDB lookup keyed by `dimm_dn` or chassis+slot; the `spl` uses `UCS_DEFAULT` until you wire that in.

**Step 3 — Validate against UCSM and CIMC**
(1) UCSM: Equipment → Server → Inventory → Memory—per-DIMM correctable should track `latest_corr_count` for the same `dimm_dn` within poll skew.
(2) CIMC-only systems are out of scope for this UCSM stats+fault pair; see `exclusions`.
(3) UCSM↔Intersight transition windows: counters may reset on rebind; the streamstats reset branch handles discontinuities—tag the change id in the ticket.
(4) If API+syslog duplicate faults, de-dupe the paging path; raw `corr_fault_count_24h` can double otherwise.

**Step 4 — Operationalize** (dashboard, alerts, pager body)
(1) Dashboard: heatmap of `corr_rate_per_hour` by `dimm_dn`×time; line of fleet 7d trend; single value of `imminent_uncorrectable` count; table of critical/warning per `dimm_health`.
(2) Alert: 15m schedule for `imminent_uncorrectable` / `severity_bucket=critical`; daily digest for `correctable_elevated` to platform (throttle `dimm_dn`).
(3) Pager / ticket body must include `dimm_dn` and `mem_slot` for FRU dispatch.

**Step 5 — Tuning and troubleshooting** (ECC gotchas)
- CIMC firmware update vs intentional clear statistics: both zero counters; `_indextime` clustering vs change record tells benign reset from RMA work.
- Single-bit / chipkill: thousands of correctable/h may still be “within vendor guardrails”—tune the lookup, not only the Splunk line.
- UCSM 4.x spelling `corrErrCnt` / `uncorrErrCnt`—extend `coalesce()` after `fieldsummary`.
- Intersight-managed blades may deprioritise legacy stats—empty here is not proof of healthy DIMMs if MCE fires at the ESXi layer.
- PMem app tag activity vs DIMM ECC—separate runbooks; see exclusions.
- HBM2 / X410c on-package paths may need an extra `classId` branch—this base `rex` is `mem-` DIMM-anchored first.
- Cleared fault semantics—pair with 19.1.1 for `lc`/`occur` if governance requires ack-aware triage on the same domain.

Closing: the `spl`’s subsearch on `cisco:ucs:faults` and the main `cisco:ucs:stats` pipeline both honour earliest=-24h@h, matching a unified lookback for dual sourcetype scope even though counter math and fault aggregation must be separated in SPL for correctness.
(Expansion — keep lookup and Cisco field notes in your runbook: document DIMM lot, BMC level, and MCE host-side triage for the same `dimm_dn` when UCSM and ESXi disagree.)

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-24h@h
| eval cidl=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, ""))
| where cidl IN ("memoryunitenvstats", "memoryarrayenvstats")
| eval correctable=tonumber(coalesce(correctable, corr_err_cnt, corrErrCnt, correrrcnt, 0))
| eval uncorrectable=tonumber(coalesce(uncorrectable, uncorr_err_cnt, uncorrErrCnt, uncorrerrcnt, 0))
| rex field=dn max_match=0 "(?<dimm_dn>(?:sys/chassis-\\d+/blade-\\d+|sys/rack-unit-\\d+)/board/memarray-\\d+/mem-\\d+)$"
| where isnotnull(dimm_dn)
| rex field=dimm_dn max_match=0 "^(?<chassis_blade>sys/chassis-\\d+/blade-\\d+)/board"
| rex field=dimm_dn max_match=0 "^(?<rack>sys/rack-unit-\\d+)/board"
| rex field=dimm_dn max_match=0 "memarray-(?<memarray>\\d+)/mem-(?<mem_slot>\\d+)$"
| bin _time span=15m
| stats latest(correctable) as correctable latest(uncorrectable) as uncorrectable by dimm_dn, _time
| sort 0 dimm_dn _time
| streamstats current=f last(correctable) as prev_corr last(uncorrectable) as prev_unc last(_time) as prev_time by dimm_dn
| eval corr_delta=if(isnotnull(correctable) AND isnotnull(prev_corr), if(correctable>=prev_corr, correctable-prev_corr, correctable), null())
| eval unc_delta=if(isnotnull(uncorrectable) AND isnotnull(prev_unc), if(uncorrectable>=prev_unc, uncorrectable-prev_unc, uncorrectable), null())
| eval bin_seconds=_time-prev_time
| eval corr_rate_per_hour=if(isnotnull(corr_delta) AND bin_seconds>0, round(corr_delta*3600/bin_seconds, 1), null())
| eval uncorr_rate_per_hour=if(isnotnull(unc_delta) AND bin_seconds>0, round(unc_delta*3600/bin_seconds, 1), null())
| eval latest_corr_count=correctable
| eval latest_uncorr_count=uncorrectable
| streamstats current=f last(corr_rate_per_hour) as prev_rate by dimm_dn
| eval rate_rise=if(isnotnull(corr_rate_per_hour) AND isnotnull(prev_rate), corr_rate_per_hour-prev_rate, null())
| sort 0 dimm_dn -_time
| dedup dimm_dn
| eval blade_model="UCS_DEFAULT"
| lookup ucs_dimm_error_thresholds blade_model OUTPUTNEW corr_rate_per_hour_warn, corr_rate_per_hour_crit, uncorr_rate_per_hour_crit, notes as threshold_notes
| fillnull value=100 corr_rate_per_hour_warn
| fillnull value=1000 corr_rate_per_hour_crit
| fillnull value=0 uncorr_rate_per_hour_crit
| join type=left max=0 dimm_dn [
  search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults" earliest=-24h@h
  | eval cidl=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, cidl, ""))
  | eval cidl=if(cidl=="" OR isnull(cidl), "faultinst", cidl)
  | where cidl="faultinst"
  | eval cause_s=lower(trim(coalesce(cause, fault_cause, "")))
  | eval code_s=coalesce(code, fault_code, "")
  | where cause_s IN ("dimm-correctable", "dimm-uncorrectable", "dimm-failure", "dimm-thermal-threshold-non-recoverable") OR code_s IN ("F0185", "F0186", "F0461")
  | rex field=dn max_match=0 "(?<dimm_dn>(?:sys/chassis-\\d+/blade-\\d+|sys/rack-unit-\\d+)/board/memarray-\\d+/mem-\\d+)"
  | where isnotnull(dimm_dn)
  | eval is_ucf=if(cause_s IN ("dimm-uncorrectable", "dimm-failure", "dimm-thermal-threshold-non-recoverable") OR code_s IN ("F0185", "F0461"), 1, 0)
  | eval is_cf=if(is_ucf=0 AND (cause_s=="dimm-correctable" OR code_s=="F0186"), 1, 0)
  | eval sev=if(lower(severity)=="critical",4,if(lower(severity)=="major" OR lower(severity)=="error",3,if(lower(severity)=="warning",2,1)))
  | stats sum(is_cf) as corr_fault_count_24h, sum(is_ucf) as uncorr_fault_count_24h, max(sev) as sev_n by dimm_dn
  | eval worst_severity_24h=case(sev_n>=4, "critical", sev_n=3, "major", sev_n=2, "warning", 1=1, "info")
]
| fillnull value=0 corr_fault_count_24h uncorr_fault_count_24h sev_n
| fillnull value="none" worst_severity_24h
| eval dimm_health=case(
    uncorr_rate_per_hour>0 OR uncorr_fault_count_24h>0, "imminent_uncorrectable",
    corr_rate_per_hour>=corr_rate_per_hour_crit, "correctable_storm",
    corr_rate_per_hour>=corr_rate_per_hour_warn, "correctable_elevated",
    isnotnull(rate_rise) AND rate_rise>0 AND isnotnull(corr_rate_per_hour) AND corr_rate_per_hour>0, "correctable_baseline_drift",
    1=1, "healthy"
  )
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    dimm_health="imminent_uncorrectable", "critical",
    dimm_health="correctable_storm", "warning",
    dimm_health IN ("correctable_elevated", "correctable_baseline_drift"), "watch",
    1=1, "ok"
  )
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical",1, severity_bucket="warning",2, severity_bucket="watch",3, 1=1,4)
| table dimm_dn, chassis_blade, rack, memarray, mem_slot, latest_corr_count, corr_rate_per_hour, latest_uncorr_count, uncorr_rate_per_hour, corr_fault_count_24h, uncorr_fault_count_24h, dimm_health, severity_bucket, sev_order, worst_severity_24h, threshold_notes, rate_rise
| sort 0 sev_order, dimm_dn
```

## Visualization

(1) **Heatmap** of `dimm_dn` × `_time` coloured by `corr_rate_per_hour` to show **DIMM**-level error **velocity** in time. (2) **Line chart** of fleet `corr_rate_per_hour` over 7d for **baseline** / **drift** review. (3) **Single value** counts of `dimm_health` in `imminent_uncorrectable` and `correctable_storm`. (4) **Table** of the operator columns from the `spl` for hardware triage, `severity_bucket` first. (5) Optional companion panel for **19.1.1** on the same dashboard for **fault**-only drill-down in the same window.

## Known False Positives

•

- **BIOS** / **CIMC** **firmware** update or a deliberate **clear** **statistics** in **UCSM** resets **cumulative** **counters**; the `if(current>=previous,…)` **delta** branch shows a one-bin discontinuity, not always a new fault — pair with a change id before **RMA** **DIMMs**.
•
- **UCSM** **cluster** or **CIMC** **clock** skew: two polls in the “wrong” order make **rate** look negative until the next good bin; compare **`_indextime` − `_time`** if **Sev1** trust is challenged.
•
- **Single-bit** **chip** failure that stays within **CPU**-correctable guardrails: very high **correctable**/hour can still be “healthy enough” in vendor eyes — the **lookup** row is a **planning** band, not a warranty; tune before paging.
•
- **Intermittent I2C/SEL** transport noise to the service processor: short spikes that **UCSM** soaks the next time you look in **Equipment → Memory** — require two bad **15m** **bins** before **FRU**.
•
- **Migrated** or **re-seated** **DIMMs** after a truck roll: **counters** start from zero; the **fault** list may not yet show a **F0186** repeat — treat the first bin after a move as a **re-baseline**.
•
- **AppDirect** or **PMem** traffic patterns next to a conventional **DIMM** on the same board can confuse operators who conflate **app**-tag **stats** with **DIMM** **ECC** — see **exclusions**.
•
- **vSphere** / **ESXi** health events or **MCE** logs at the **guest** for the same time window: correlated story but out of scope here — this UC is the **UCSM**-materialised view only.
•
- **Dual-feed** (API and **syslog**) to the same index can duplicate a **faultInst** row; **count**-based fields may double if you have not de-duplicated the **fault** path — use one **authoritative** feed for paging, or de-dupe in a **provisional** **summary** with `dedup` on `code`+`dimm_dn` in a 120s **window**.
•
- **Memory** **thermal** throttling and **bandwidth** loss without rising **UCSM** **ECC** **fault** entries can still hurt apps — pair with **host**-side and **19.1.34**-style thermal context when guests complain with clean **DIMM** **rates**.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Cisco UCS (Splunkbase 2731)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2731)
- [Cisco UCS Faults Reference (codes and causes, including memory-related faults)](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/unified_computing/ucs/ts/faults/reference/4-2/UCSFaultsErrorsRef_4-2.html)
- [Splunk — streamstats (cumulative counter delta and windowed per-dn series)](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/SearchReference/Streamstats)
- [Cisco UCS Manager XML API — managed objects (memory and statistics class context)](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/unified_computing/ucs/sw/api/b_UCS-XML-API-Reference_UCS-Manager-4-2/b_UCS-XML-API-Reference_UCS-Manager-4-2_chapter_0100.html)
